name: CCode Rust Agent
description: Wire existing 10 Rust crates into a fully-featured AI coding agent CLI per all 15 spec docs, from core UX polish through MCP, KAIROS, Bridge, ULTRAPLAN, TEAMMEM, and Multi-Agent orchestration.

userStories.title: REPL multi-turn conversation mode
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run `ccode repl` and have a persistent multi-turn conversation so that I can iterate on tasks without restarting.
acceptanceCriteria: `ccode repl` starts an interactive prompt loop
Each input round uses the same session, preserving full conversation history
Supports `/exit` or Ctrl-D to quit
Supports `/clear` to reset session history
Session auto-persists to file via FileSessionRepo
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Implement in crates/cli/src/cmd/repl.rs. Use bootstrap::wire_from_config_with_cwd() to get AppState, then loop stdin → AgentRunCommand::run() with same session_id.

userStories.title: Streaming output formatting
userStories.description: As a user, I want streaming output to display cleanly with proper formatting so that the experience feels polished.
acceptanceCriteria: LLM delta tokens print incrementally without extra newlines or garbled text
Tool call start displays tool name and parameter summary
Tool result displays completion status (success/failure)
Error messages show clear category (auth / network / rate limit)
Markdown code blocks display correctly in terminal
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Touch crates/cli/src/cmd/agent.rs streaming delta handler. Consider a small render helper for markdown formatting.

userStories.title: Tool confirmation UX optimization
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clear tool approval UX so that I can quickly allow or deny tool executions.
acceptanceCriteria: Before tool execution, display: tool name, parameters, risk level
Support `y` (allow), `n` (deny), `a` (always allow this tool in session)
Always-allowed tools skip confirmation for the rest of the session
Denial continues the agent loop without aborting the conversation
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Existing agent.rs has basic confirmation. Enhance the prompt formatting and add 'a' (always) option with a HashSet<String> tracker.

userStories.title: Session management CLI commands
userStories.description: As a user, I want to list, view, and delete sessions so that I can manage my conversation history.
acceptanceCriteria: `ccode sessions list` shows session summaries (ID, timestamp, message count)
`ccode sessions show <id>` displays full conversation content
`ccode sessions delete <id>` removes the specified session
`ccode sessions clear` deletes all sessions (with confirmation prompt)
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Implement in crates/cli/src/cmd/sessions.rs. Use SessionRepository trait from ports.

userStories.title: Cron management CLI commands
userStories.description: As a user, I want to manage cron jobs via CLI so that I can schedule recurring agent tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: `ccode cron list` displays all scheduled tasks
`ccode cron create --schedule '...' --message '...'` creates a task
`ccode cron delete <id>` removes a task
`ccode cron run <id>` manually triggers one execution
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Implement in crates/cli/src/cmd/cron.rs. Use CronRepository from ports and FileCronRepo from cron crate.

userStories.title: Unified error handling and user messages
userStories.description: As a user, I want consistent error messages across all commands so that I can understand what went wrong.
acceptanceCriteria: All crate error types convert to user-friendly messages at CLI layer
Network errors suggest checking connectivity
Auth errors suggest checking API key configuration
Missing config suggests config.example.toml location
Unexpected errors include correlation context (session id, provider name)
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Add a display_error() helper in cli crate that pattern-matches on AppError/WireError/ConfigError variants and prints user-friendly output.

userStories.title: Canonical LLM types in domain crate
userStories.description: As a developer, I want canonical LLM types (ContentBlock, LlmRequest, LlmResponse, StreamEvent) defined in domain so that all crates share one source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: ContentBlock enum with variants: Text, ToolUse, ToolResult (per llm-compat-contract.md)
LlmRequest / LlmResponse defined in domain, replacing CompletionRequest/Response in ports
StopReason enum: EndTurn, MaxTokens, ToolUse, StopSequence
StreamEvent enum: ContentDelta, ToolCallDelta, MessageComplete
TokenUsage struct: input_tokens, output_tokens
All types derive Serialize, Deserialize, Debug, Clone
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Add new module crates/domain/src/llm.rs. These are the Canonical types from llm-compat-contract.md Section 2.

userStories.title: LlmClient trait replacing ProviderPort
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single LlmClient trait replacing the current ProviderPort so that the interface matches llm-compat-contract.md.
acceptanceCriteria: LlmClient trait with: complete(&self, req: LlmRequest) -> Result<LlmResponse, LlmError>
LlmClient trait with: stream(&self, req: LlmRequest) -> Result<LlmStream, LlmError>
LlmError enum: Auth, RateLimit, Timeout, Network, InvalidRequest, ProviderError
LlmClient defined in ports crate, replaces ProviderPort
All downstream consumers (application, tools, bootstrap) updated to use LlmClient
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Rename/refactor ProviderPort → LlmClient in crates/ports/src/provider.rs. Update all references across application, tools, bootstrap, cli.

userStories.title: Provider adapter triple conversion
userStories.description: As a developer, I want provider adapters to convert between Canonical and wire formats so that no provider-specific types leak above the provider crate.
acceptanceCriteria: AnthropicAdapter converts Canonical → Anthropic request and Anthropic → Canonical response
OpenAiCompatAdapter converts Canonical → OpenAI request and OpenAI → Canonical response
Tool definition bidirectional conversion (Canonical schema ↔ provider schema)
Streaming events convert from provider SSE to Canonical StreamEvent
All adapters implement the new LlmClient trait
Router implements LlmClient (failover/round-robin logic preserved)
Upper crates (application, tools) only use Canonical types
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Refactor crates/provider/src/compat/, openai_compat/, anthropic_compat/ to use Canonical types from domain. Update router.rs.

userStories.title: MCP runtime crate — transport layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a new mcp-runtime crate that handles MCP protocol transport so that the agent can connect to external MCP servers.
acceptanceCriteria: New crates/mcp-runtime added as workspace member
Stdio transport implemented (spawn child process, stdin/stdout JSON-RPC)
McpTransport trait with: send(), receive(), close()
Connection failure returns clear error classification
Unit tests for message serialization/deserialization
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Create crates/mcp-runtime/. Implement stdio transport first per chicago-mcp-contract.md. SSE transport can come later.

userStories.title: MCP tool discovery and registration
userStories.description: As a user, I want the agent to discover tools from MCP servers and make them available for use.
acceptanceCriteria: On startup, read MCP server list from config
Call tools/list on each server to get tool definitions
Convert MCP tools to ToolPort interface and register in ToolRegistry
MCP tool execution forwards via tools/call to the corresponding server
Server disconnect auto-removes its tools from registry
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Add MCP config section to crates/config. Wire discovery in bootstrap. Create McpToolAdapter implementing ToolPort.

userStories.title: MCP reserved name and capability policy
userStories.description: As a developer, I want reserved MCP server names and capability checks enforced per chicago-mcp-contract.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Reserved server names (e.g. 'computer') rejected with policy error
Privileged adapter (computer-use) requires feature gate to enable
Tool turn-end cleanup: temporary resources cleaned after each agent turn
Capability negotiation: only enable capabilities the server declares support for
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Add reserved name list and policy check in mcp-runtime. Wire cleanup hook into agent loop in application crate.

userStories.title: KAIROS AssistantModeService implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want KAIROS mode resolution and prompt composition so that the agent can operate in different assistant modes.
acceptanceCriteria: AssistantModeService trait implemented in application crate
resolve_mode(): determines effective mode from config + session state
build_prompt(): composes system prompt by precedence order
route_input(): slash command routing (e.g. /brief switches mode)
Mode switch emits a DomainEvent
Unit tests for mode resolution logic
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per kairos-contract.md. Add mode types to domain, service to application. Prompt precedence must be deterministic and testable.

userStories.title: Mode-aware tool visibility
userStories.description: As a user, I want tool visibility to change based on the current assistant mode so that irrelevant tools are hidden.
acceptanceCriteria: Each mode defines a tool allowlist
ToolRegistry supports filtered view by mode
Brief mode hides high-risk tools
Mode switch takes effect immediately without restart
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Add definitions_for_mode(mode) method to ToolRegistry. Wire into agent loop's tool list construction.

userStories.title: ModeCoordinatorService implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want mode coordination logic per coordinator-mode-contract.md so that env mode and session mode are reconciled deterministically.
acceptanceCriteria: ModeCoordinatorService trait implemented
resolve_effective_mode(): session mode takes precedence over env mode
reconcile_on_resume(): handles mode conflict when resuming a session
Mode switch event carries metadata (from, to, reason)
Invalid transitions return InvalidModeTransition error
Unit tests for precedence and reconciliation logic
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per coordinator-mode-contract.md. Implement in application crate. Env var: CLAUDE_CODE_COORDINATOR_MODE.

userStories.title: TriggerSchedulerService local scheduling
userStories.description: As a user, I want to schedule durable agent tasks that survive process restart so that I can automate recurring workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: TriggerSchedulerService trait: create, list, delete
TriggerTask supports SessionOnly and Durable scope
Durable tasks persist to state-store
Owner model: MainAgent / Teammate / TeamLead
Teammate cannot create Durable tasks (returns DurableNotAllowedForTeammate)
Delete checks owner identity before removing
Unit tests for ownership and scope rules
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per agent-triggers-contract.md. Add trigger types to domain, service to application, storage to session/cron crate.

userStories.title: RemoteTriggerDispatchService
userStories.description: As a developer, I want remote trigger dispatch so that scheduled tasks can execute on remote infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: RemoteTriggerDispatchService trait: dispatch(payload) -> String
Feature gate controls availability (gate off returns GateDisabled)
Requires auth/org context validation
Transport errors do not leak internal details
Local scheduling works independently when remote is disabled
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per agent-triggers-contract.md. Depends on remote-runtime crate existing. Wire feature gate in config.

userStories.title: Remote runtime crate — RemoteSessionService
userStories.description: As a developer, I want bridge mode remote session management per bridge-mode-contract.md.
acceptanceCriteria: New crates/remote-runtime added as workspace member
RemoteSessionService trait: create, fetch, archive, update_title, reconcile_resume
RemoteSessionState state machine: Pending -> Running -> Idle -> Archived/Expired/Failed
Forbidden transitions enforced: Archived -> Running, Expired -> Running
Archive is idempotent (409 treated as success)
Unit tests for state transitions
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per bridge-mode-contract.md. Create crates/remote-runtime/. State types go in domain, service trait in application or ports.

userStories.title: CcrClient HTTP transport layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an HTTP client for remote session API communication.
acceptanceCriteria: CcrClient trait: HTTP transport for remote session lifecycle
Session ID prefix translation (session_ vs cse_) handled only in remote-runtime
Auth token and org context auto-attached to requests
Timeout and retry configurable via config
CcrClientError and RemoteSessionError as separate error layers
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per bridge-mode-contract.md. Implement in crates/remote-runtime/src/client.rs. Use reqwest.

userStories.title: UltraplanService state machine
userStories.description: As a user, I want to launch long-running planning sessions on remote infrastructure with lifecycle management.
acceptanceCriteria: UltraplanService trait: launch, poll, stop, archive_orphan
8-state machine: Idle -> Launching -> Polling -> AwaitingInput -> Approved -> Completed/Failed
At most one active ULTRAPLAN per session context
Launch while already Launching/Polling returns AlreadyActive
Failure after remote session creation auto-attempts orphan archive
Stop clears local active marker before returning
Unit tests for state transitions and guard conditions
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per ultraplan-contract.md. Implement in application crate, using remote-runtime for transport.

userStories.title: Platform crate — secure path validation and secret scanning
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a platform crate for secure path validation and filesystem abstractions needed by TEAMMEM.
acceptanceCriteria: New crates/platform added as workspace member (or integrated into existing crate)
validate_canonical_path(): rejects '..', symlink escape, paths outside root
SecretScanner: scans file content for secret patterns
Scan results include rule_id and label for auditing
Unit tests for path traversal and symlink attack scenarios
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per teammem-contract.md. This is a prerequisite for TEAMMEM sync. Keep it focused: path validation + secret scan only.

userStories.title: TeamMemorySyncService implementation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to sync team memory with remote storage with security guarantees.
acceptanceCriteria: TeamMemorySyncService trait: pull, push, sync
Pull -> merge -> push with ETag / 412 conflict retry (bounded)
All remote writes pass secret scan first
All local writes pass canonical path validation first
Path traversal / symlink escape returns PathValidationFailed
Secret detected skips file with audit metadata
File over max-size skipped with classification
Retry exhausted returns ConflictExhausted
Unit tests for security invariants
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per teammem-contract.md. Invariant ordering: validate -> scan -> write/push. Implement in application crate, using platform + remote-runtime.

userStories.title: MultiAgentOrchestrator implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want parallel agent orchestration per multi-agent-orchestration-contract.md.
acceptanceCriteria: MultiAgentOrchestrator trait: spawn_parallel, handle_notification, synthesize_summary, stop_task
WorkerTaskSpec includes criticality (Blocking / Sidecar)
Parallel fan-out rejects overlapping write scopes
Worker prompts are self-contained with ownership boundaries
Coordinator produces synthesized summary (no raw pass-through)
Stop is idempotent — stopping a terminal-state task does not error
Unit tests for fan-out policy and synthesis
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per multi-agent-orchestration-contract.md. Implement in application crate. Uses tokio::spawn for parallel workers.

userStories.title: AgentTool and TaskStopTool integration
userStories.description: As a user, I want the coordinator agent to spawn and manage worker agents via tools.
acceptanceCriteria: AgentTool supports spawning workers with WorkerTaskSpec
TaskStopTool supports stopping a running worker
Worker results reported as WorkerResultNotification
Notification routing to coordinator's handle_notification
UI displays worker status (Running / Completed / Failed)
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Extend existing crates/tools/src/spawn_agent.rs. Add TaskStopTool. Wire notification routing in bootstrap.

userStories.title: Cross-feature integration contracts enforcement
userStories.description: As a developer, I want cross-feature integration contracts enforced per integration-contracts.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Bridge x Ultraplan: remote session APIs only through app-services
MCP x Tool Runtime: privileged adapter controlled by tool runtime hooks
TeamMem x KAIROS: memory prompts do not bypass secret/path safety
Triggers x Remote: local scheduling works when remote is disabled
Coordinator x Multi-Agent: deterministic fan-out policy
Unified error envelope: policy / auth / transport / state / validation categories with correlation ID
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per integration-contracts.md. This is a verification/wiring story — add integration tests that assert boundary rules.

userStories.title: Observability baseline
userStories.description: As an operator, I want structured logging and trace IDs across all runtime crates.
acceptanceCriteria: All async operations have tracing spans
Correlation ID propagates from CLI entry to provider call
Error logs include structured context (session_id, provider, tool_name)
RUST_LOG env var controls log level
Sensitive data (API keys, tokens) never appears in logs
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Add tracing spans to application, provider, tools, mcp-runtime, remote-runtime. Add correlation ID to AppState or pass via tracing context.

userStories.title: IME correctness for CJK input
userStories.description: As a user inputting Chinese, I want correct IME handling in the REPL.
acceptanceCriteria: Preedit / commit separation handled correctly
Grapheme-aware backspace (does not delete half a character)
Display width cursor alignment (fullwidth chars occupy two columns)
Pressing Enter during IME composition does not submit incomplete text
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per acceptance-spec.md IME requirements. May need unicode-width and unicode-segmentation crates. Test with CJK input in REPL.

userStories.title: Security audit and hardening
userStories.description: As a developer, I want security controls verified before release.
acceptanceCriteria: cargo audit reports no known vulnerabilities
Secret scanning covers all TEAMMEM and config paths
Tool sandbox permissions default to deny (fail-closed)
Remote endpoints do not leak internal error details
All feature gates default to off
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per acceptance-spec.md security requirements. Run cargo audit, verify deny-by-default in sandbox config, check error messages in remote-runtime.
